body:
## Implementation Plan Complete

I've completed a comprehensive implementation plan for scene context metadata (POV, location, timeframe) with filterable navigator.

### Plan Summary

**Scope:** 4 new components, 6 modified files across 6 implementation phases

**Key deliverables:**
- Three optional metadata fields on Scene type (backward compatible)
- MetadataRow component in editor for in-context editing
- MetadataFilters component in corkboard toolbar
- Metadata display in scene cards
- AND-logic filter combination (match all active filters)
- Empty states and graceful fallbacks

**Critical requirements:**
1. Optional fields - support existing scenes without metadata
2. Filter logic: AND combination (scene must match ALL active filters)
3. Quick-edit in cards optional for v1 (can defer to v2)
4. Grayscale tokens only

**Phase order:** Data model → Editor → Filters → Card display → Polish → Responsive

### Design Brief Alignment

The plan incorporates all Design brief requirements:
- ✅ Metadata row in editor (POV, Location, Timeframe) below title
- ✅ Filter controls in corkboard `toolbarRight`
- ✅ Metadata display in scene cards (compact chips)
- ✅ Keyboard accessible inputs and dropdowns
- ✅ Grayscale token usage
- ✅ Responsive collapse (mobile stacks metadata fields vertically)

### Test Plan Included

Comprehensive coverage:
- Unit tests for updateSceneMetadata and filter logic
- Integration tests for persistence and filtering
- Manual keyboard/accessibility validation
- Edge case handling (missing metadata, long values, empty filter results)

### Next Steps

The plan is ready for implementation. Code Monkey should proceed phase-by-phase and note that quick-edit in scene cards is marked optional for v1 if time-constrained. See full plan in issue description.

Assign to: Code Monkey

## UX goal
Give writers instant narrative orientation while drafting by keeping POV, location, and timeframe visible and editable in-context, with one-click filtering in the scene navigator so continuity checks do not interrupt writing flow.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: current scene title + scene metadata chips (POV, location, timeframe) at the top of the editor.
2. Secondary: navigator filter bar (POV, location, timeframe + clear all) and filtered scene count.
3. Tertiary: per-scene quick edit controls in scene cards/settings for metadata maintenance.
4. Supporting: helper text and empty-state guidance when metadata is missing or filters return zero scenes.

## Layout and components
- Editor mode (`src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`): keep existing `editorHeader` structure, add a metadata row directly below title input and above editor canvas.
- Metadata row: three compact field groups (`POV`, `Location`, `Timeframe`) using existing input/select visual language; labels remain always visible.
- Corkboard mode: extend `Corkboard` toolbar with filter controls on the right side (currently reserved in `toolbarRight`).
- Scene cards (`SceneCard`): show metadata as a compact three-item line between summary and footer; truncate long values with tooltip/title.
- Quick edit: in scene card footer, add a lightweight “Edit context” action opening inline fields or a compact popover (choose whichever fits current CSS module approach best).

## Interaction states
- Idle: metadata displayed for current scene; filter chips/selects show current values.
- Loading: reuse existing corkboard skeleton; for metadata row show neutral placeholders in `--color-fog-200` blocks.
- Empty: if scene lacks metadata, show muted placeholders like “Add POV”; if filters produce no scenes, show explicit empty-state copy plus `Clear filters` action.
- Error: inline field-level error text below affected metadata input; keep entered value visible; non-blocking tone.
- Success: save is silent/low-friction; optional subtle “Saved” text in metadata row for 1-2s after edit.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: all metadata fields and filter controls tabbable in logical order (title -> metadata -> editor/filter bar).
- Focus: preserve existing 2px focus outline pattern (`--color-carbon-600`) on new controls.
- Contrast: text and borders must stay within current grayscale tokens with readable contrast (primary text on white/polar backgrounds).
- Text clarity: use explicit labels (`POV`, `Location`, `Timeframe`) not placeholder-only inputs.

## Visual style
- Keep current grayscale system only (`src/styles/tokens.css`):
  - Surfaces: `--color-white`, `--color-polar-100`
  - Borders/dividers: `--color-fashion-300`
  - Primary text: `--color-black-700`
  - Secondary/meta text: `--color-elephant-500` / `--color-cement-400`
- Maintain existing calm, minimal look; avoid introducing accent colors for v1 metadata.
- Preserve typography rhythm: sans UI labels/controls; keep editor body serif treatment unchanged.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop: metadata row appears as one horizontal row with three equal fields; corkboard filters remain in toolbar right area.
- Tablet: metadata fields wrap to two rows if needed; toolbar filters may wrap but stay above grid.
- Mobile: metadata fields stack vertically with full-width controls; filter controls collapse to a compact row with horizontal scroll or stacked controls; keep one-tap clear action visible.

## Codebase alignment
- Reviewed architecture and style primitives:
  - `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`
  - `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx`
  - `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.module.css`
  - `src/components/SceneCard/SceneCard.tsx`
  - `src/components/SceneCard/SceneCard.module.css`
  - `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/*`
  - `src/components/AppShell/*`, `Header/*`, `Navigation/*`, `ContentContainer/*`
  - `src/styles/tokens.css`
  - `docs/INSPIRATION.md`

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse:
  - Existing story page header/layout containers.
  - Existing corkboard toolbar and scene card structure.
  - Existing token palette, spacing, borders, focus treatments.
- New build:
  - Scene metadata row component/section in editor mode.
  - Navigator filter controls for metadata fields.
  - Scene metadata display + quick-edit affordance in cards.

## Feasibility notes
- Current scene model (`src/types/scene.ts`) does not yet include metadata fields, so UI should tolerate undefined values gracefully.
- Data is localStorage-backed (`src/lib/scenes.ts`) and synchronous; design should assume immediate optimistic updates and no heavy async workflow in v1.
- Filtering needs combined criteria logic; ensure clear visual indication of active multi-filter state.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Keep changes anchored to existing modules rather than introducing a new global design system layer.
- Preferred anchors:
  - Editor metadata row: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` + `page.module.css`.
  - Corkboard filter bar: `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx` (`toolbarRight`) + module CSS.
  - Card metadata/quick-edit: `src/components/SceneCard/SceneCard.tsx` + module CSS.
  - Types/storage extension points: `src/types/scene.ts`, `src/lib/scenes.ts`.
- Preserve one-click mode switching behavior from `ViewModeSwitch` and avoid adding modal-heavy flows that interrupt drafting.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This feature should be approved because it can differentiate on **workflow speed inside drafting**, not just data modeling: scene context (POV/location/timeframe) is visible and editable where writing happens, and filter combinations let writers resolve continuity questions without leaving the manuscript flow. That is a better experience than forcing writers into separate planning/worldbuilding surfaces.

## Competitor evidence
- Living Writer emphasizes structure, scenes, and planning boards (including scene/location plot points), which shows metadata-driven organization is expected baseline behavior: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/boards/planning-board/freeform-grid and https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/boards/planning-board/standard-grid
- Living Writer navigation focus (tabbed chapters) indicates fast movement across manuscript is a core competitive axis; our filterable navigator can outperform by adding context-aware retrieval, not only tab switching: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/tabbed-viewing
- Campfire’s positioning is module-heavy worldbuilding (encyclopedia/locations/magic/species), which is powerful but can push users into parallel panels; we can stand out with lighter, inline context controls while drafting: https://campfirewriting.com/worldbuilding-tools
- Dabble links scene cards and plot points to manuscript workflow, reinforcing that context + scene-level retrieval is market-proven value: https://help.dabblewriter.com/en/articles/2692382-exploring-dabble-s-plot-grid

## Risks / trade-offs
- Free-text metadata can fragment values (e.g., “NYC” vs “New York”), weakening filters unless lightweight normalization/suggestions are included.
- Added header metadata could increase visual noise if not carefully designed for focus mode.
- If filter UX is slow or hidden, this devolves into parity instead of differentiation.

## Recommendation
Proceed, but keep the differentiator explicit in Design brief: prioritize **single-glance context + one-step filtering** in the drafting flow, and include lightweight value suggestions to reduce metadata drift.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
